3559	
3560	% --- (D) The section-localization descent: the gap1 keystone ---
3561	
3562	\begin{lemma}\leanok
3563	[Section-localization descent from a cover hypothesis (gap1 keystone, D, cover form)]
3564	  \label{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover}
3565	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover}
3566	  \uses{lem:map_units_restrict_basicOpen, lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib,
3567	    lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib, lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib,
3568	    lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib}
3569	  % NOTE: this is the LANDED, axiom-clean keystone (Lean decl
3570	  % isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_cover EXISTS). It takes the per-cover-element localization
3571	  % data Hfr as a HYPOTHESIS and assembles all three IsLocalizedModule fields by pure finite-cover
3572	  % sheaf theory; it does NOT route through the global QCoh ≃ Mod equivalence (= gap1 itself). The
3573	  % NAMED form lem:section_localization_descent is this lemma instantiated at the quasi-coherence
3574	  % cover once Hfr is produced (lem:pullback_gamma_top_iso).
3575	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", \S "Sections over principal opens",
3576	  % Lemma \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections} (read from references/stacks-properties.tex, L2152-L2171).
3577	  % Cf. [Hartshorne], II.5.3.
3578	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
3579	  % "Let $X$ be a scheme. Let $f \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$.
3580	  %  Denote $X_f \subset X$ the open where $f$ is invertible, see
3581	  %  Schemes, Lemma \ref{schemes-lemma-f-open}.
3582	  %  If $X$ is quasi-compact and quasi-separated, the canonical map
3583	  %  $$
3584	  %  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)_f \longrightarrow \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{O}_X)
3585	  %  $$
3586	  %  is an isomorphism. Moreover, if $\mathcal{F}$ is a quasi-coherent
3587	  %  sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules the map
3588	  %  $$
3589	  %  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})_f \longrightarrow \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{F})
3590	  %  $$
3591	  %  is an isomorphism."
3592	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", \S ``Sections over principal opens'',
3593	  Lemma \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections}; cf.\ [Hartshorne], II.5.3.}
3594	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) and \(f \in R\). Suppose given a finite basic-open
3595	  cover of \(\Spec R\): a finite set \(t \subseteq R\) with \(\mathrm{span}(t) = (1)\), so that
3596	  \(\{D(r)\}_{r \in t}\) covers \(\Spec R\). Suppose moreover the \emph{per-piece localization data}
3597	  \[
3598	    \mathrm{Hfr} : \quad \text{for every open } U \le D(r) \text{ for some } r \in t, \quad
3599	    \bigl(\Gamma(M, U) \longrightarrow \Gamma(M,\, D(f) \sqcap U)\bigr)
3600	    \text{ is } \mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f),
3601	  \]
3602	  i.e.\ on every open contained in a cover member -- in particular each \(D(r)\) and each overlap
3603	  \(D(r) \sqcap D(r')\) -- the restriction to the \(f\)-locus exhibits the target as the localization
3604	  of the source at the powers of \(f\). Then the global section restriction
3605	  \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is itself \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\)
3606	  over \(R\); that is, \(\Gamma(M, D(f)) \cong (\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\,\Gamma(M, \top)\).
3607	\end{lemma}
3608	% NOTE: the lemma STATEMENT is cited to Stacks lemma-invert-f-sections / Hartshorne II.5.3; the proof
3609	% below is the project-internal finite-cover descent (gluing + separatedness + flatness of
3610	% localization) that deliberately does NOT route through the global affine equivalence (which is gap1
3611	% itself), so there is no transcribed `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF`.
3612	\begin{proof}
3613	  \uses{lem:map_units_restrict_basicOpen, lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib,
3614	    lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib, lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib,
3615	    lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib}
3616	  We verify the three fields of \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) for the restriction
3617	  \(\theta : \Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\).
3618	
3619	  \emph{\(\mathrm{map\_units}\).} For every \(n\) the element \(f^n\) acts invertibly on
3620	  \(\Gamma(M, D(f))\): on the basic open \(D(f)\) the global section \(f\) is a unit of
3621	  \(\Gamma(\mathcal{O}, D(f)) = R_f\), and scalar multiplication by a unit is bijective. This holds
3622	  for an arbitrary sheaf of modules \(M\) and is \cref{lem:map_units_restrict_basicOpen}; it does not
3623	  use \(\mathrm{Hfr}\).
3624	
3625	  \emph{\(\mathrm{surj}\) (every section over \(D(f)\) is a fraction).} Let
3626	  \(y \in \Gamma(M, D(f))\). On each cover member \(D(r)\), the restriction
3627	  \(y|_{D(f) \sqcap D(r)}\) lies in the localization \(\Gamma(M, D(f) \sqcap D(r))\) of
3628	  \(\Gamma(M, D(r))\) at \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\) (the case \(U = D(r)\) of \(\mathrm{Hfr}\)), so there
3629	  are \(x_r \in \Gamma(M, D(r))\) and \(n_r\) with \(f^{n_r}\, y|_{D(f)\sqcap D(r)} = x_r|_{D(f)\sqcap
3630	  D(r)}\). Taking a common exponent \(N \ge \max_r n_r\) (the cover is finite) and rescaling, we may
3631	  assume \(f^{N} y\) is represented on each \(D(r)\) by a section \(\tilde x_r\). On an overlap
3632	  \(D(r) \sqcap D(r')\) the two representatives \(\tilde x_r, \tilde x_{r'}\) restrict to sections of
3633	  \(\Gamma(M, D(r) \sqcap D(r'))\) that agree after further multiplication by \(f\): both compute
3634	  \(f^{N} y\) on \(D(f) \sqcap D(r) \sqcap D(r')\), and the overlap restriction
3635	  \(\Gamma(M, D(r)\sqcap D(r')) \to \Gamma(M, D(f) \sqcap D(r) \sqcap D(r'))\) is
3636	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) (the case \(U = D(r) \sqcap D(r')\) of
3637	  \(\mathrm{Hfr}\)), hence injective up to a power of \(f\); so there is a common further exponent
3638	  \(P\) with \(f^{P}\tilde x_r\) and \(f^{P}\tilde x_{r'}\) equal on the overlap. The family
3639	  \(\{f^{P}\tilde x_r\}_r\) is then compatible on overlaps and glues, by the sheaf condition over the
3640	  finite cover (\cref{lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}), to a global section
3641	  \(x \in \Gamma(M, \top)\). Its restriction to \(D(f)\) equals \(f^{N+P} y\): the two agree on each
3642	  \(D(f) \sqcap D(r)\), and \(\{D(f)\sqcap D(r)\}_r\) covers \(D(f)\), so they agree by separatedness
3643	  (\cref{lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}). Thus \(\theta(x) = f^{N+P} y\), exhibiting \(y\) as the
3644	  fraction \(x / f^{N+P}\).
3645	
3646	  \emph{\(\mathrm{exists\_of\_eq}\) (a vanishing fraction is annihilated by a power of \(f\)).}
3647	  Let \(x \in \Gamma(M, \top)\) with \(\theta(x) = x|_{D(f)} = 0\). On each cover member \(D(r)\), the
3648	  restriction \(x|_{D(r)}\) maps to \(0\) in \(\Gamma(M, D(f) \sqcap D(r))\), so by the localization
3649	  hypothesis (the case \(U = D(r)\) of \(\mathrm{Hfr}\), which gives the \(\mathrm{exists\_of\_eq}\)
3650	  field on that piece) there is \(n_r\) with \(f^{n_r}\, x|_{D(r)} = 0\). A common exponent
3651	  \(N \ge \max_r n_r\) over the finite cover gives \(f^{N} x|_{D(r)} = 0\) for every \(r\); since the
3652	  \(D(r)\) cover \(\Spec R\), separatedness (\cref{lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}) forces
3653	  \(f^{N} x = 0\) in \(\Gamma(M, \top)\).
3654	
3655	  These three fields are exactly \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) for \(\theta\).
3656	  Equivalently the comparison \((\mathrm{powers}\,f)^{-1}\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is an
3657	  isomorphism -- the \(\Gamma(X, \mathcal{F})_f \cong \Gamma(X_f, \mathcal{F})\) of Stacks
3658	  \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections} on \(X = \Spec R\), \(X_f = D(f)\), obtained from local data over a
3659	  finite cover by sheaf gluing and separatedness, with the finite limit interacting with localization
3660	  through the flatness of localization (\cref{lem:isLocalization_flat_mathlib},
3661	  \cref{lem:isLimitPullbackCone_mathlib}), never through the global affine equivalence.
3662	\end{proof}
3663	
3664	% --- (D, engines) The private sheaf-gluing sub-steps of the cover-form descent (coverage) ---
3665	
3666	\begin{lemma}
3667	[Restriction maps of a sheaf compose (descent bookkeeping)]
3668	  \label{lem:res_comp}
3669	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.res_comp}
3670	  \textit{Project-local bookkeeping for the section-localization descent.}
3671	  For a sheaf \(F\) on \(\Spec R\) and opens \(C \le B \le A\), the composite of the restriction maps
3672	  \(\Gamma(F, A) \to \Gamma(F, B) \to \Gamma(F, C)\) equals the direct restriction
3673	  \(\Gamma(F, A) \to \Gamma(F, C)\).
3674	\end{lemma}
3675	\begin{proof}
3676	  The two poset inclusions \(C \le B \le A\) compose to \(C \le A\) in the poset of opens (uniqueness
3677	  of poset-hom), and \(F.\mathrm{map}\) is functorial, so the two presheaf maps compose to the direct
3678	  one.
3679	\end{proof}
3680	
3681	\begin{lemma}
3682	[A finite spanning family covers $\Spec R$ by basic opens (descent bookkeeping)]
3683	  \label{lem:iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top}
3684	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top}
3685	  \uses{lem:isLocalization_basicOpen_mathlib}
3686	  \textit{Project-local glue for the sheaf-gluing reduction of the descent.}
3687	  If a finite family \(t \subseteq R\) spans the unit ideal, \(\mathrm{span}(t) = (1)\), then the
3688	  supremum of the basic opens \(D(r)\) over \(r \in t\) is \(\top\); i.e.\ \(\{D(r)\}_{r \in t}\) is a
3689	  finite basic-open cover of \(\Spec R\).
3690	\end{lemma}
3691	\begin{proof}
3692	  Standard: \(\bigsqcup_{r \in t} D(r) = \top\) iff the \(r\) generate the unit ideal
3693	  (\(\mathrm{PrimeSpectrum.iSup\_basicOpen\_eq\_top\_iff}\)), applied to \(\mathrm{span}(t) = (1)\).
3694	\end{proof}
3695	
3696	\begin{lemma}
3697	[Overlap representatives agree after a power of $f$ (descent surjectivity sub-step)]
3698	  \label{lem:descent_overlap_agree}
3699	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.descent_overlap_agree}
3700	  \uses{lem:res_comp}
3701	  \textit{Project-local sub-step of the cover-form descent.}
3702	  In the surjectivity argument of \cref{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover}: given a section
3703	  \(y\) over \(D(f)\) and, on a cover member \(D(r)\), a representative \(\tilde x_r\) with
3704	  \(f^N y = \tilde x_r|_{D(f)\sqcap D(r)}\), the restrictions of two such representatives
3705	  \(\tilde x_r, \tilde x_{r'}\) to the overlap \(D(r) \sqcap D(r')\) become equal after multiplication
3706	  by a common power of \(f\).
3707	\end{lemma}
3708	\begin{proof}
3709	  Both \(\tilde x_r\) and \(\tilde x_{r'}\) compute \(f^N y\) on \(D(f) \sqcap D(r) \sqcap D(r')\); the
3710	  overlap restriction \(\Gamma(M, D(r)\sqcap D(r')) \to \Gamma(M, D(f) \sqcap D(r) \sqcap D(r'))\) is a
3711	  localization at \(\mathrm{powers}\,f\) (the overlap case of \(\mathrm{Hfr}\)), hence injective up to a
3712	  power of \(f\); compatibility of the restriction maps is \cref{lem:res_comp}.
3713	\end{proof}
3714	
3715	\begin{lemma}
3716	[Surjectivity field of the cover-form descent]
3717	  \label{lem:descent_surj}
3718	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.descent_surj}
3719	  \uses{lem:descent_overlap_agree, lem:res_comp, lem:iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top,
3720	    lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib, lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}
3721	  \textit{Project-local: the \(\mathrm{surj}\) engine of the cover-form descent.}
3722	  Given the per-cover-element localization data \(\mathrm{Hfr}\) on a finite basic-open cover, every
3723	  section \(y \in \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is, up to a power of \(f\), the restriction of a global section: there
3724	  are \(x \in \Gamma(M, \top)\) and \(n\) with \(x|_{D(f)} = f^n\, y\).
3725	\end{lemma}
3726	\begin{proof}
3727	  Per cover member, \(\mathrm{Hfr}\) at \(U = D(r)\) gives a representative of \(f^{n_r} y\); a common
3728	  exponent \(N\) (finite cover, \cref{lem:iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top}) yields representatives
3729	  \(\tilde x_r\), which agree on overlaps after a further power of \(f\)
3730	  (\cref{lem:descent_overlap_agree}). The patched family glues
3731	  (\cref{lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}) to \(x \in \Gamma(M, \top)\), and \(x|_{D(f)} = f^{N+P} y\) by
3732	  separatedness over the cover \(\{D(f)\sqcap D(r)\}\) (\cref{lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}), the
3733	  restrictions composing by \cref{lem:res_comp}.
3734	\end{proof}
3735	
3736	\begin{lemma}
3737	[Separatedness/torsion field of the cover-form descent]
3738	  \label{lem:descent_smul_eq_zero}
3739	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.descent_smul_eq_zero}
3740	  \uses{lem:iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top, lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib, lem:res_comp}
3741	  \textit{Project-local: the \(\mathrm{exists\_of\_eq}\) engine of the cover-form descent.}
3742	  Given the per-cover-element localization data \(\mathrm{Hfr}\), any global section
3743	  \(x \in \Gamma(M, \top)\) with \(x|_{D(f)} = 0\) is annihilated by a power of \(f\): there is \(n\) with
3744	  \(f^n\, x = 0\).
3745	\end{lemma}
3746	\begin{proof}
3747	  Per cover member, \(\mathrm{Hfr}\) at \(U = D(r)\) (its \(\mathrm{exists\_of\_eq}\) field) kills
3748	  \(x|_{D(r)}\) by some \(f^{n_r}\); the finite sup of these powers
3749	  (\cref{lem:iSup_basicOpen_subtype_eq_top}) kills every \(x|_{D(r)}\), and separatedness over the cover
3750	  (\cref{lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}, restrictions composing by \cref{lem:res_comp}) lifts this to
3751	  \(f^n\, x = 0\) globally.
3752	\end{proof}
3753	
3754	% --- (D, basic-open cover form) The form gap1 actually consumes ---
3755	
3756	\begin{lemma}
3757	[Section-localization descent from a BASIC-open cover hypothesis (gap1 keystone, D, basic-open form)]
3758	  \label{lem:section_localization_descent_of_basicOpen_cover}
3759	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen_descent_of_basicOpen_cover}
3760	  \uses{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover, lem:descent_surj, lem:descent_smul_eq_zero,
3761	    lem:map_units_restrict_basicOpen, lem:existsUnique_gluing_mathlib,
3762	    lem:eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}
3763	  % NOTE: this is the form gap1 USES. Same conclusion as the general-U cover form
3764	  % lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover, but Hfr is demanded only at BASIC opens D(s) ≤ D(r).
3765	  % The general-U form is axiom-clean, but its Hfr (a localization on EVERY open U ≤ D(r)) cannot be
3766	  % discharged for an arbitrary quasi-coherent M — a general open of Spec R_r need not be quasi-compact.
3767	  % The sheaf-gluing engines descent_surj/descent_smul_eq_zero only ever consult Hfr at basic opens
3768	  % D(s) and overlaps D(r·r') = D(r)⊓D(r') (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul), all quasi-compact, so the
3769	  % basic-open hypothesis suffices to rebuild all three IsLocalizedModule fields.
3770	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], "Properties of Schemes", \S ``Sections over principal opens'',
3771	  Lemma \texttt{lemma-invert-f-sections}; cf.\ [Hartshorne], II.5.3.}
3772	  Let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules on \(\Spec R\) and \(f \in R\). Suppose given a finite basic-open
3773	  cover of \(\Spec R\): a finite set \(t \subseteq R\) with \(\mathrm{span}(t) = (1)\). Suppose moreover
3774	  the \emph{basic-open} per-cover-element localization data
3775	  \[
3776	    \mathrm{Hfr} : \quad \text{for every } s \in R \text{ with } D(s) \le D(r) \text{ for some } r \in t,
3777	    \quad \bigl(\Gamma(M, D(s)) \longrightarrow \Gamma(M,\, D(f) \sqcap D(s))\bigr)
3778	    \text{ is } \mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f).
3779	  \]
3780	  Then the global section restriction \(\Gamma(M, \top) \to \Gamma(M, D(f))\) is itself
3781	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f)\) over \(R\).
3782	\end{lemma}
3783	\begin{proof}
3784	  \uses{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover, lem:descent_surj, lem:descent_smul_eq_zero,
3785	    lem:map_units_restrict_basicOpen}
3786	  A thin wrapper over the cover-form descent. The three \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}\) fields are rebuilt
3787	  exactly as in \cref{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover}, but every appeal to the per-piece data
3788	  is at a basic open: \(\mathrm{map\_units}\) is \cref{lem:map_units_restrict_basicOpen} (independent of
3789	  \(\mathrm{Hfr}\)); \(\mathrm{surj}\) is \cref{lem:descent_surj}, whose only \(\mathrm{Hfr}\) call is at
3790	  the cover member \(D(r) = D(r)\) itself; and \(\mathrm{exists\_of\_eq}\) is
3791	  \cref{lem:descent_smul_eq_zero}, again calling \(\mathrm{Hfr}\) only at each \(D(r)\). The overlaps it
3792	  consults are \(D(r) \sqcap D(r') = D(r\cdot r')\) (a basic open, by
3793	  \(\mathrm{PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen\_mul}\)), so the basic-open hypothesis \(\mathrm{Hfr}\) supplies every

3796	  \(U \le D(r)\), which is not available for an arbitrary quasi-coherent \(M\); this basic-open form is
3797	  the one gap1 uses.)

3815	  % named-form descent and gap1 become "one-liners" once this lands: they do NOT. The prover (iter-036
3816	  % task_results/QuotScheme) established that gammaPullbackTopIso is an Ab (additive-group) iso whose
3817	  % underlying map is only Γ(X,U)-semilinear over the SOURCE-scheme ring, whereas Hfr is an
3818	  % IsLocalizedModule statement R-linear over R. TWO Mathlib-absent ingredients remain between
3819	  % gammaPullbackTopIso and Hfr: (I) a ring-iso-semilinear IsLocalizedModule transport (Mathlib only
3820	  % has same-ring of_linearEquiv), and (II) a base-change-of-localization R -> R_r bridge. Project-

3822	  \textit{Project-bespoke infrastructure: the section-level transport feeding the per-cover-element
3823	  hypothesis \(\mathrm{Hfr}\) of \cref{lem:section_localization_descent_of_cover}.}
3824	  Let \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion of schemes and let \(M\) be a sheaf of modules
3825	  on \(X\). The global sections of the pullback module along \(j\) are canonically isomorphic to the
3826	  sections of \(M\) over the image of \(j\):
3827	  \[
3828	    \Gamma\bigl((\mathrm{pullback}\,j).\mathrm{obj}\,M,\ \top\bigr)
3829	    \;\cong\; \Gamma\bigl(M,\ \mathrm{range}\,j\bigr),
3830	  \]
3831	  naturally in the open argument: for opens \(V \le \top\) of \(U\) the comparison
3832	  \(\Gamma((\mathrm{pullback}\,j).\mathrm{obj}\,M, V) \cong \Gamma(M, j(V))\) intertwines the
3833	  restriction maps of the two presheaves, so any two such instances commute with the restriction
3834	  between them.

3837	\begin{proof}

3840	  For an open immersion \(j\), Mathlib's restriction functor \(j^{\mathrm{res}}\) satisfies
3841	  \(\Gamma(j^{\mathrm{res}} M, V) = \Gamma(M, j(V))\) \emph{definitionally}
3842	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctor_mathlib}); in particular at \(V = \top\) the right-hand side is
3843	  \(\Gamma(M, j(\top)) = \Gamma(M, \mathrm{range}\,j)\). The restriction functor is naturally
3844	  isomorphic to the universal-property pullback,
3845	  \(j^{\mathrm{res}} \cong j^{*} = \mathrm{pullback}\,j\)
3846	  (\cref{lem:modules_restrictFunctorIsoPullback_mathlib},
3847	  \cref{lem:modules_pullback_mathlib}). Applying the global-sections functor \(\Gamma(-, V)\) to the
3848	  component of this natural isomorphism at \(M\) gives, for each open \(V\), an isomorphism
3849	  \(\Gamma((\mathrm{pullback}\,j).\mathrm{obj}\,M, V) \cong \Gamma(j^{\mathrm{res}} M, V)
3850	  = \Gamma(M, j(V))\); naturality of the functor isomorphism in \(V\) makes these commute with the
3851	  presheaf restriction maps. Taking \(V = \top\) yields the displayed isomorphism with
3852	  \(\Gamma(M, \mathrm{range}\,j)\).
3853	\end{proof}

3962	  \label{lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_transport}

3966	  % Role: the algebra/category-free CORE that the geometric Hfr producer

3969	  \textit{Project-bespoke: the combiner of bridges (I)~\cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_ringEquiv_semilinear}
3970	  and (II)~\cref{lem:isLocalizedModule_restrictScalars_powers_algebraMap}.}
3971	  Let \(\sigma : S \xrightarrow{\sim} A\) be a ring isomorphism with \(A\) an \(R\)-algebra, and let
3972	  \(f \in R\), \(f' \in S\) satisfy \(\sigma(f') = \mathrm{algebraMap}\ R\ A\ f\). Let
3973	  \(g : M_1 \to M_2\) be an \(S\)-linear map with \(\mathrm{IsLocalizedModule}(\mathrm{powers}\,f')\ g\),

3975	  isomorphisms onto \(A\)-modules \(N_i\) that are also \(R\)-modules with
3976	  \(\mathrm{IsScalarTower}\ R\ A\ N_i\). If \(h : N_1 \to N_2\) is \(A\)-linear and intertwines them,
3977	  \(h(e_1 x) = e_2(g x)\), then the restriction of scalars of \(h\) to an \(R\)-linear map satisfies

4262	\begin{lemma}[Range and $f$-locus computations for the composite immersion (producer, b)]
4263	  \label{lem:composite_immersion_range_basicOpen}
4264	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.composite_immersion_range_basicOpen}
4265	  \uses{def:gamma_image_ring_equiv}
4266	  \textit{Project-bespoke: image/range bookkeeping of the geometric Hfr producer.}
4267	  With \(j : \Spec R_s \to \Spec R\) the composite immersion of
4268	  \cref{lem:pullback_composite_immersion_isIso_fromTildeΓ}, let
4269	  \(\sigma = \mathrm{gammaImageRingEquiv}\,j\,\top : \Gamma(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec R_s}, \top) \xrightarrow{\sim}
4270	  \Gamma(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec R}, j\,''^{U}\top)\) be the \(\top\)-level structure-sheaf ring iso
4271	  (\cref{def:gamma_image_ring_equiv}), and put \(f' := \sigma^{-1}(\mathrm{algebraMap}\,R\,R_s\,f)\). Then
4272	  \[
4273	    j.\mathrm{opensRange} = D(s), \qquad j\,''^{U}\,D(f') = D(f) \sqcap D(s), \qquad
4274	    \sigma(f') = \mathrm{algebraMap}\,R\,R_s\,f .
4275	  \]
4276	\end{lemma}
4277	\begin{proof}
4278	  The range identity \(j.\mathrm{opensRange} = D(s)\) is the open-immersion range of the composite of the
4279	  basic-open immersion \(\iota_W\), the affine-chart immersion \(\iota_{q.X_i}\), and the affine
4280	  identification \(\mathrm{isoSpec}.\mathrm{inv}\) (each an open immersion onto a basic / affine open),
4281	  whose composite image is exactly \(D(s)\). The third identity \(\sigma(f') = \mathrm{algebraMap}\,R\,R_s\,f\)
4282	  is definitional from \(f' = \sigma^{-1}(\mathrm{algebraMap}\,R\,R_s\,f)\). The middle identity computes
4283	  the image of the basic open \(D(f')\) under \(j\) on the structure-sheaf side through \(\sigma\): the
4284	  ring iso carries \(f'\) to \(\mathrm{algebraMap}\,R\,R_s\,f\), the image of \(f\), and the image of a
4285	  basic open is the basic open of the image element intersected with the range, giving
4286	  \(D(f) \sqcap D(s)\).

Now I need to identify the specific isolated `lem:gr_...` node. Looking at the 6 new GrassmannianCells blocks, `def:gr_liftToBaseOfMemRange` and `lem:gr_det_one_updateCol` both have NO `\uses{}` edges. `lem:gr_det_one_updateCol` specifically states "For a vector v... the determinant of the identity matrix with its p-th column replaced by v is the entry v_p" — and is then USED by `lem:gr_free_entry_eq_signed_minor`. 

Checking the blueprint confirms that `lem:gr_det_one_updateCol` has no `\uses{}` directive in either its statement or proof block, making it the isolated node I'm looking for. Looking at the remaining lemmas, `lem:gr_rotMid` pulls in a bunch of definitions about cocycles and away multiplication, `lem:gr_transitionInvImageMatrix` and `lem:gr_transitionInvPair` both have substantial `\uses{}` declarations, so they're all connected. That leaves `lem:gr_det_one_updateCol` as the only one with no `\uses{}` at all — truly isolated. Though `def:gr_liftToBaseOfMemRange` also has no `\uses{}` on itself, it's still referenced by another lemma, so it has incoming edges even if it doesn't depend on anything. So `def:gr_liftToBaseOfMemRange` has inbound edges from `lem:gr_algebraMap_comp_liftToBaseOfMemRange`, but `lem:gr_det_one_updateCol` appears to be completely isolated — nothing references it and it references nothing, which might indicate it's genuinely not proved or is an orphaned lemma.

The `✗` marking in leandag for isolated nodes means there's no `\leanok` directive in the blueprint. Since `sync_leanok` would add that directive if the Lean declaration exists without `sorry`, the issue might be that these are `private` declarations that aren't being matched properly — they'd need their fully qualified namespace-prefixed names like `AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rotMid` to be recognized.

Looking at the leandag summary showing 309 proved nodes and 9 with `sorry`, the 6 GrassmannianCells blocks likely match their Lean declarations (since there are 0 GR entries in the unmatched list), but they're missing `\leanok` either because they contain `sorry` statements or because `sync_leanok` hasn't processed them yet. The 6 new GrassmannCells coverage blocks exist in Lean as private lemmas but haven't been processed by `sync_leanok` yet since they were just added to the blueprint—they'll correctly receive the `\leanok` tag after the next sync run. Now I need to identify which node is isolated by checking if `lem:gr_det_one_updateCol` has any incoming `\uses{}` edges from other blueprint nodes, since it appears to have no outgoing dependencies itself. So the proof of `lem:gr_free_entry_eq_signed_minor` explicitly describes the determinant calculation using the `det_one_updateCol` concept, but that lemma isn't listed in the `\uses{}` block—only `def:gr_universal_matrix` and `def:gr_minor_det` are cited. This is the missing dependency I need to flag.

Now I'm checking whether `def:gr_liftToBaseOfMemRange` appears in the proof of `lem:gr_existence_factor_through_valuation_ring`, and looking at its `\uses{}` declaration which references `lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul` and `lem:gr_existence_minimal_valuation` among others.

Since `def:gr_liftToBaseOfMemRange` has no `\uses{}` block itself but `lem:gr_algebraMap_comp_liftToBaseOfMemRange` depends on it, this definition has at least one incoming edge and isn't isolated. That means `lem:gr_det_one_updateCol` is the isolated node — it has no dependencies listed and nothing references it, so the fix would be to add it to some `\uses{}` block. The proof text describes how the map factors through the subring, which is exactly what `def:gr_liftToBaseOfMemRange` encapsulates, yet this definition isn't listed in the `\uses{}` even though the Lean proof calls it. I notice the definition appears after the lemma in the file, which might explain why it's missing from the dependencies.

For the isolated `lem:gr_det_one_updateCol` node, I should add it to the `\uses{}` of `lem:gr_free_entry_eq_signed_minor` — that's a wire-up task with soon severity. The other isolated node `lem:annihilator_meets_nonZeroDivisors` is already marked as `\mathlibok`, so I'll keep that as is.

Now I need to scan through FlatBaseChange.tex and FlatteningStratification.tex to catch any remaining issues, focusing on the key sections I haven't fully reviewed yet.

`Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex` private declarations (confirmed matched in leandag), plus

- 4 QUOT section-transport producer nodes: `lem:pullback_composite_immersion_isIso_fromTildeΓ` **(sub-gap a)**, `lem:composite_immersion_range_basicOpen`, `lem:gamma_image_iso_semilinear_top`, and `def:sec...` (likely `section_localization_hfr_basicOpen`-related) — all dependencies resolved.

### 1. Picard_QuotScheme.tex

**`complete: true`** | **`correct: true`** | **HARD GATE: PASSES**

#### Re-routing of `lem:section_localization_descent` ✓

The proof block of `lem:section_localization_descent` (lines ~4139–4178) correctly routes through

No must-fix or soon findings in Picard_QuotScheme.tex. The chapter is coherently staged: all
